title: Bridge Essay: The Neural Mechanisms of Private Speech in Second Language Learners' Oral Produc

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Language as mantra — mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention.
**Phase context:** Mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, language as mantra.

**The Neural Mechanisms of Private Speech in Second Language Learners' Oral Production: An fNIRS Study.** — Brain sciences (2025). DOI: 10.3390/brainsci15050451. PMID: 40426622. Background: According to Vygotsky's sociocultural theory, private speech functions both as a tool for thought regulation and as a transitional form between outer and inner speech. However, its role in adult second language (L2) learning-and the neural mechanisms supporting it-remains insufficiently understood.
